question: Operators flagged a short window of unusual top-of-winding temperature readings on T-014 over the last few days. First identify the sensor channels installed on this asset, then run an anomaly check and trend analysis on the winding temperature channel (winding_temp_top_c) to determine whether the deviations form a single burst or a sustained shift.
effective_question: Operators flagged a short window of unusual top-of-winding temperature readings on T-014 over the last few days. First identify the sensor channels installed on this asset, then run an anomaly check and trend analysis on the winding temperature channel (winding_temp_top_c) to determine whether the deviations form a single burst or a sustained shift. What is the definition of a 'short window'? Are there any known maintenance or operational changes on T-014 over the last few days?

clarifying_questions: What is the definition of a 'short window'?
Are there any known maintenance or operational changes on T-014 over the last few days?
assumptions: The sensor channels installed on T-014 are standard and well-documented.
The anomaly check and trend analysis will be performed using a standard statistical method.
The winding temperature channel (winding_temp_top_c) is correctly calibrated and functioning properly.

answer: Based on the results, the unusual top-of-winding temperature readings on T-014 over the last few days appear to be a single burst of anomalies rather than a sustained shift. The anomaly check identified two readings with high z-scores (3.673 and 3.408) within a short window, indicating a brief deviation from the normal behavior. The trend analysis showed a stable trend with a slight positive slope, indicating no sustained shift in the winding temperature over the observed period.
